data.title: Hillenbrand Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83, revenue of $652M
data.description:
• Hillenbrand press release (HI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83.
  • Revenue of $652M.
  • FY 2025 net revenue of $2.67 billion decreased 16% vs. prior year; pro forma net revenue decreased 9%
  • FY 2025 GAAP EPS of $0.61 increased from $(3.03) in the prior year; adjusted EPS of $2.49 decreased 25%

data.title: Agilent Technologies raises dividend by ~3%
data.description:
• Agilent Technologies (A) declared $0.255/share quarterly dividend, 2.8% increase from prior dividend of $0.248.
  • Forward yield 0.71%
  • Payable Jan. 28; for shareholders of record Jan. 6; ex-div Jan. 6.
  • See A Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Coinbase said to launch Kalshi-powered prediction market
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) is set to roll out a Kalshi (KALSHI)-powered prediction marketplace to let its customers bet on various events, sports and elections, according to a media report on Wednesday.

The crypto giant plans to unveil details about the new prediction market and additional offerings during an event on Dec. 17, The Information reported, citing one of two people familiar with the matter. Prediction market Kalshi (KALSHI) reportedly allows users to deposit Circle Internet Group's (CRCL) USDC (USDC-USD) stablecoin and relies on Coinbase (COIN) to hold those tokens in custody.

The trading platform's entry into prediction markets comes amid growing interest in the space. Retail trading app Robinhood Markets (HOOD) has already launched a prediction market, and derivatives exchange Cboe Global Markets (CBOE) was said to be rolling one out soon.

Coinbase did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Vizsla Silver announces proposed offering of $250M
data.description:
• Vizsla Silver (NYSE:VZLA) announced  on Wednesday an offering of $250M convertible senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The company expects to grant the initial purchasers an additional $50M amount of notes for a period of 13 days, beginning on and including the date on which the notes are first issued.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds  to support the exploration and development of the Panuco Project, potential future acquisitions, and for general corporate purposes.
  •  Furthermore, the company plans to pay the purchase price for the capped call transactions with a portion of the net proceeds from the offering or from existing cash on hand. 
  • The notes will mature on January 15, 2031, unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased. 
  • In connection with the offering, the company expects to enter into privately negotiated cash-settled capped call transactions with one or more of the initial purchasers of the notes, their respective affiliates, and other financial institutions.
  • The capped call transactions are expected generally to compensate for potential economic dilution upon conversion of the notes.

data.title: Nano Dimension GAAP EPS of -$0.13, revenue of $26.9M
data.description:
• Nano Dimension press release (NNDM): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.13.
  • Revenue of $26.9M (+80.5% Y/Y).

  • Year-to-date 2025 Results:

    • Revenue: $67.1 million, a 55.4% increase from $43.2 million year-over-year
    • Gross Margin (“GM”): 31.3%, down from 46.5% year-over-year
    • Adjusted Gross Margin (“Adjusted GM”): 45.5%, down from 48.4% year-over-year
    • Adjusted EBITDA loss: $43.3 million, from a loss of $44.7 million year-over-year
    • Net Loss from Continuing Operations: $66.4 million, down from a loss of $90.5 million year-over-year

    • For the fourth quarter of 2025, the Company anticipates revenue in the range of $31.5 million to $33.5 million, non-GAAP gross margin of 47% to 48.5%, non-GAAP operating expenses of $28 million to $29 million, and Adjusted EBITDA loss in the range of $12 million to $14 million.

data.title: Kulicke and Soffa Non-GAAP EPS of $0.28, revenue of $177.5M
data.description:
• Kulicke and Soffa press release (KLIC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.28.
  • Revenue of $177.5M.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Highlights

    • Net revenue of $654.1 million.
    • Gross margin of 42.5%.
    • Net income of $0.2 million or $0.004 per fully diluted share; non-GAAP net income of $11.0 million or $0.21 per fully diluted share.
    • GAAP cash from operations of $113.6 million; Adjusted free cash flow of $96.6 million.
    • The Company repurchased a total of 2.4 million shares of common stock at a cost of $96.5 million.
    • Cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments were $510.7 million as of October 4, 2025.

data.title: Jack In The Box Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 misses by $0.17, revenue of $326.2M beats by $2.42M
data.description:
• Jack In The Box press release (JACK): FQ4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 misses by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $326.2M (-6.6% Y/Y) beats by $2.42M.
  • Shares -9.62%.

  • Guidance & Outlook

    The following guidance and underlying assumptions reflect the company’s current expectations for the fiscal year ending September 27, 2026. All guidance represents the standalone Jack in the Box brand unless otherwise noted. Del Taco results will be reflected in discontinued operations for fiscal year 2026. As the company restructures following the sale of Del Taco, the company expects variability across quarters, as indicated below:

    Fiscal Year 2026 Company-wide Guidance

    • Jack in the Box Restaurant Count of 2,050 to 2,100
      • This includes approximately 20 new restaurant openings and approximately 50 to 100 closures, most of which will be franchise restaurants
    • Same Store Sales of -1% to +1% vs. Fiscal Year 2025
      • The company expects first-quarter results to remain pressured, with sequential improvement anticipated over the balance of fiscal year 2026
    • Company-Owned Restaurant Level Margin of 17 to 18%
      • This includes mid-single-digit commodity inflation and low-single-digit wage inflation
    • Franchise Level Margin of $275 to $290 million
      • As the company continues to execute its “Jack on Track” plan, which includes a block closure program and selling real estate, both of which influence Franchise Level Margin, visibility into timing is limited.
    • SG&A of $125 to $135 million
      • G&A, excluding selling and advertising, is expected to be approximately 2.5% of systemwide sales. This will remain elevated for the first half of the year and then improve into the back half of the year as the company restructures following the sale of Del Taco
      • This does not include any offset from income as part of the Transition Services Agreement (“TSA Income”) that is expected to be received as part of the Del Taco divestiture
    • Depreciation and Amortization of $45 to $50 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $225 to $240 million
    • Capital Expenditures of $45 to $55 million, prioritizing sales-driving investments in technology
    • As previously mentioned, the company has discontinued its dividend and share repurchase program.

data.title: Datadog drops for seventh straight session
data.description:
Datadog (DDOG) shares extended their losing streak to a seventh consecutive session, as the stock fell 0.11% to close at $176.26 on Wednesday.

Datadog’s slide comes even though the company recently posted strong Q3 results and lifted its full-year forecast. The company also shared the revenue and EPS outlook for the fourth quarter, both above consensus estimates and reported more large customers in its latest update.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, Datadog holds a Hold score of 3.42 out of 5. The company earned an A in revisions, A- in profitability and momentum, and B in growth, while valuation was marked F.

Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish on the stock.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 40 out of 46 analysts rate DDOG with Buy or higher, while six rated it Hold.

An analyst said Datadog delivered a “standout Q3,” pointing to a 53% jump in backlog and strong demand for its AI-native products, adding, “the surge in Q3 backlog was spectacular, which shows how much room revenues can grow ahead.”

Shares have gained around 12% over the past month and are up around 25% year to date, significantly outperforming the broader market.

data.title: Wall Street finished higher despite a volatile topsy turvy trading session
data.description:
Wall Street closed out Wednesday's trading session on a high note despite the zigzagging up and down that the markets experienced.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) concluded higher by 0.5%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished up 0.5% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) rounded out the day up in the green by 0.3%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, six of the 11 S&P segments closed in positive territory as Info Tech led the way. At the other end of the spectrum, Energy had suffered the most on the day as oil (CL1:COM) dropped 2.1%.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced Wednesday that it is canceling the release of the delayed October jobs report, citing an inability to collect survey data amid the ongoing government shutdown.

Treasury yields inched up as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved higher by 1 basis point to 4.13% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up by 1 basis point to 3.58%. 

The Fed minutes were released and they highlighted the widening divide among its members over whether the policymakers need to lower rates further or not. And it doesn't look like the disagreement will resolve by the December meeting.

“Minutes released by the central bank on Wednesday showed that there is uncertainty regarding whether or not a further rate cut should be implemented next month. Although this is far from a guaranteed outcome, I would say that worsening economic conditions in the labor market and broader economic weakness, will force a rate cut in December,” Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones said.

“Long term, this is a positive thing, and the market understands this. But near term, I think that this optimism is misplaced as there are legitimate worries about an AI bubble and the recession in the US economy is almost guaranteed to enter into in the coming months,” Jones added.

After two days of risk-off selling on AI valuation and spending concerns, investors will likely continue to be cautious until Nvidia (NVDA) reports after the bell. 

Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid highlighted that Nvidia has now fallen more than 12% from its October 29 peak — its sharpest drop since the “Liberation Day” volatility earlier this year. 

"Nvidia is the holy grail on which the market bases its mood – such a concentrated focus on one sector ((AI)) has practically never been seen before in this form. Valuations are hot, as are expectations. In short: caution, yes – panic, no," Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker, said on X. 

Elsewhere on the earnings front, Target (TGT) ended -2.7% after the retailer reported a decline in quarterly sales. Conversely, Lowe's (LOW) finished +4% after the home improvement retailer raised its annual sales guidance. 

Additionally, the publication of delayed economic data after the government shutdown continued on Wednesday. The trade deficit in U.S. goods and services narrowed more than expected in August.
